name: context-compaction
description: Claude session runs one normal turn to populate context, then the driver triggers /compact to compact the conversation history, then runs a second normal turn to prove the session continues working after compaction. Recording may be brittle: newer Claude Code (2.1.x+) writes a NEW transcript file at the compaction boundary, which can register as a fresh session row to irrlicht depending on session-stitching (see session-resume cell). Per assessment.json, irrlicht's CompactionState domain field is dead code — the dashboard cannot distinguish compaction from a normal long turn. The recipe therefore asserts only the visible state arc (ready→working→ready on each side of /compact).

metadata.notes:
agent_supports: no
daemon_capability: n/a
driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: no
daemon_capability: n/a
driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

The scenario's primitive is `context_curation` / a **compaction command** (e.g. Claude Code `/compact`): a user-invoked, in-place summarization that produces a visible `ready → working → ready` arc. aider has no such command.

- No `/compact` (or equivalent) slash command exists. aider 0.86.2's full command set (`def cmd_*` in `commands.py`) is: add, architect, ask, chat_mode, clear, code, commit, context, copy, copy_context, diff, drop, edit, editor, editor_model, exit, git, help, lint, load, ls, map, map_refresh, model, models, multiline_mode, paste, quit, read_only, reasoning_effort, report, reset, run, save, settings, test, think_tokens, tokens, undo, voice, weak_model, web. The closest are `/clear` and `/reset` (discard history outright) and `/tokens` / `/drop` (report or shrink context) — none summarize the conversation in place via an LLM call, which is what the scenario asserts. The official in-chat-commands docs confirm "No `/compact` command exists."
- aider DOES have an analogous capability, but it is **automatic and background-threaded**, not a user-invoked command. `ChatSummary.too_big()` (`history.py`) trips once chat history crosses `--max-chat-history-tokens` (arg help: "Soft limit on tokens for chat history, after which summarization begins"), and `summarize_start()` (`base_coder.py:1002`) spawns a `threading.Thread` that summarizes via the **weak** model off the REPL's main path. The REPL never blocks on it and never enters a distinct foreground state for it. Because the scenario as written requires a deliberate compaction *command* with a foreground `working` arc, the honest verdict against the canonical primitive is `agent_supports: no` — the automatic mechanism is documented in the caveat below rather than upgraded to `yes`.

## daemon_capability: n/a

Derived as n/a because the agent lacks the command-form feature (`agent_supports: no`), so there is no observation question to answer. For completeness: even the automatic summarizer would leave no trace in the watched Source. The aider adapter (`agent.go`) is `FilesUnderCWD{Filename: ".aider.chat.history.md", RawLineParser}` — the parser only sees lines written to that markdown file: `####` user prompts, assistant prose, `> Tokens:` / `> Model:` / `> Applied edit` / `> Running` blockquotes, and LLM-error blockquotes (`parser.go`). The summarizer never calls `io.append_chat_history` (all `append_chat_history` callers in `io.py` are user-prompt / assistant-output / tool-output writes); it only swaps the in-memory `done_messages`. The verbose `"Starting/Finished summarizing chat history"` strings go to terminal `tool_output`, not the history file. So no event would land in the watched transcript, and the parser would emit no state arc for compaction — a `daemon_capability: incapable` would be the verdict if aider's automatic summarizer were the in-scope feature, but since the command-form feature is absent the axis is `n/a`.

## driver_capability: ready

Frozen because `agent_supports: no` — no recording follows. Per the #476 re-derivation convention, the driver axis records `ready`: `scripts/drive-aider-interactive.sh` already has every step type a hypothetical compaction recipe would need (`send`, `slash`, `wait_turn`, `sleep`), so the freeze is owned by the absent agent feature, not a tooling gap.

## What would flip the verdict

If aider shipped a user-invoked `/compact`-style command that wrote a recognizable marker to `.aider.chat.history.md`, `agent_supports` would become `yes` and `daemon_capability` could be re-judged against that marker (today it would be `incapable` because the summarizer writes nothing to the history file).
caveats: aider DOES auto-summarize chat history once it exceeds --max-chat-history-tokens (ChatSummary in history.py), but it runs in a background thread via the weak model with no foreground working arc and no user command — so it is not the scenario's compaction-command primitive.

The auto-summarizer writes nothing to .aider.chat.history.md, so even if the scenario were re-scoped to automatic compaction it would be unobservable (daemon_capability: incapable) with today's FilesUnderCWD-on-markdown transport.

sources.note: Full in-chat command list; confirms no /compact command — /clear and /reset discard history, /tokens and /drop manage context but do not summarize
aider 0.86.2 cmd_* set has no cmd_compact; closest are cmd_clear / cmd_reset / cmd_tokens / cmd_drop
ChatSummary.too_big() + summarize() — automatic, weak-model summarization of chat history
summarize_start() at line 1002 spawns a background threading.Thread; the REPL never enters a foreground working state for it
--max-chat-history-tokens help: 'Soft limit on tokens for chat history, after which summarization begins'
Source is FilesUnderCWD{.aider.chat.history.md, RawLineParser} — parser only sees lines in the markdown history file; would be daemon_capability: incapable for the auto-summarizer (no trace written)
Recognized line kinds: #### prompts, > Tokens/Model/Applied edit/Running, error blockquotes — no compaction marker; turn arcs come from these + IdleFlush

metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

Claude Code supports both `/compact` (user-initiated) and auto-compaction when the context window fills. Newer Claude Code versions (≥ 2.1.x) emit a new transcript file at the compaction boundary; the pre-compaction transcript closes and a fresh `<new-uuid>.jsonl` starts with a synthesized 'summary of prior conversation' user-like message. The agent_supports answer is yes.

### What irrlicht observes (the parts that work)

During `/compact` itself the runtime is calling its summarizer model — Claude Code emits assistant_streaming events (no terminal stop_reason yet) and possibly a small wrapper user event for the slash-command invocation (the parser already skips `<local-command…>` / `<command-name>` wrappers via `isLocalCommandContent`, parser.go:216). The session therefore sits in `working` for the full compaction duration: rule 4 default keeps it working as long as transcript activity continues without a terminal stop_reason. When the summarizer finishes and writes a terminal assistant message with `stop_reason=end_turn` (or a `system/turn_duration` event mapped to `turn_done`), `IsAgentDone()` fires and rule 2b returns `working → ready`. That matches the spec's first three bullets:

- `ready → working` at compaction start: parser sees the slash-command wrapper / first assistant_streaming event, classifier rule 4 transitions to working.
- `working` for entire compaction: assistant_streaming events keep `IsAgentDone()` returning false (resolveAssistantStreaming downgrades non-terminal stop_reasons to `assistant_streaming`, parser.go:288). No flicker to ready or waiting.
- `working → ready` when compaction completes: the synthesized terminal message + turn_done event triggers rule 2b.

### What irrlicht does NOT (yet) observe

The domain has the `CompactionState` field (CompactionStateCompacting / CompactionStatePostCompact at session.go:16-18) but no current code path sets it. Grep confirms zero writers in `core/application/services/` or `core/pkg/tailer/` — the field only appears in golden testdata as `not_compacting`. So irrlicht currently treats compaction as an opaque long working turn; the compaction-vs-normal-turn distinction is invisible to the dashboard.

A more subtle gap: newer Claude Code versions create a NEW transcript file at the compaction boundary, then continue writing in it. irrlicht's fswatcher would treat this as a fresh session (new UUID stem = new SessionID). The continuity-of-state guarantee the spec implies — same session row stays `working` across compaction — depends on the daemon's session-stitching path (the `session-resume` cell exercises this). If stitching is incomplete on the compaction boundary the dashboard could show the old row flipping to `ready` (via session-removal sweep when its file closes) AND a new row appearing in `working` — still satisfies the underlying state arc, but only across two sessions.

The last spec bullet ('state stays `ready` immediately after — no further transitions until the user sends a new prompt') is straightforward: post-compaction transcripts are quiet until the next user message, so rule 4 has nothing to fire on.

### Why 'partial' not 'yes'

The core spec bullets (state stays working through compaction, lands at ready) are observed via the same JSONL-event path as a normal long turn. But:

1. Compaction-vs-normal-turn is not distinguishable in irrlicht's data model (CompactionState is unused).
2. The new-transcript-at-boundary path is implementation-dependent on session stitching working correctly for the boundary.

The spec doesn't require irrlicht to label compaction explicitly, so the strict reading is closer to 'yes'. But because the spec talks about staying working for the ENTIRE compaction duration, and the new-file boundary creates a discontinuity that has its own scenario (session-resume), partial reflects honest residual transport risk.
caveats: irrlicht's `CompactionState` field exists in the domain (session.go:16-18) but no production code path writes to it; the dashboard cannot distinguish a compaction working-episode from a normal long working-turn. This is fine for the spec contract (which only asserts state arcs) but means richer 'show compaction in UI' work is a separate ticket.

Newer Claude Code (2.1.x+) writes a new transcript file at the compaction boundary. The state-continuity assertion ('working for the entire compaction duration') becomes a session-stitching problem at that boundary — covered by the session-resume scenario, not this one.
Recipe must distinguish user-triggered `/compact` from auto-compaction triggered by context-fill. The state arc is the same; only the trigger differs. The simpler recipe is `/compact`-driven.

sources.note: Lines 215-225 (`isLocalCommandContent`) skip the `<local-command>` wrapper Claude Code writes for slash commands; lines 282-303 (`resolveAssistantStreaming`) keep the session in `working` across the multi-step summarizer output by downgrading non-terminal stop_reasons to `assistant_streaming`.

Lines 16-18 declare CompactionStateNotCompacting/Compacting/PostCompact; line 568 renders it as `state(compacting)` in `StringState` — but no production writer sets it, so the dashboard always sees `not_compacting`.
Rule 4 default keeps a session in `working` as long as transcript activity continues without `IsAgentDone()` firing — the basis for the 'no flicker mid-compaction' guarantee.
Comment at line 3 confirms `/compact` is one of the events Claude Code fires the statusline command on, evidence that the feature ships in current builds.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key)
irrlichd is recording (--attach against a daemon with --record, or isolated mode)
setup: Driver generates a fresh lowercased session UUID and a fresh cwd under <staging>/cwd/

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok
Reply with exactly the word: still-here

verify: events.jsonl shows the UUID session entering ready after launch
events.jsonl shows ready→working→ready for the first turn
after /compact runs, the session settles back to ready (no stuck working, no waiting)
events.jsonl shows ready→working→ready for the post-compaction turn
final session state is ready
transcript.jsonl contains at least 2 user lines and at least 2 assistant lines (pre- and post-compaction turns); a compaction boundary marker may or may not be present depending on claude version

metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

Codex 0.130.0 ships a `/compact` slash command. The native binary (codex-darwin-arm64 vendor build) contains the user-facing help string "Use /compact when the conversation gets long to summarize history and free up context.", a `thread/compact/start` app-server method, a `thread/compacted` notification, and a `contextCompaction` thread-item type. So agent_supports is unambiguously yes.

### What irrlicht observes (confirmed by live probe)

A live probe (codex driven via tmux, 2026-05-23) sent a normal prompt, waited for the turn, then sent `/compact` as a plain send (text + Enter). The rollout JSONL showed `/compact` runs as a SECOND, ordinary turn in the SAME rollout file (same session_id):

- Turn 1 (normal): `event_msg/task_started` (21:34:17.190Z) → assistant message → `event_msg/task_complete` (21:34:18.779Z). ~1.6s.
- Compaction turn: `event_msg/task_started` (21:34:39.814Z) → `type:"compacted"` history-replacement record → `event_msg/context_compacted` → `event_msg/task_complete` (21:34:48.110Z). ~8.3s.

The codex parser (parser.go:155-172) maps `event_msg/task_complete` → `turn_done`, which makes `IsAgentDone()` fire and the classifier emit `working → ready`. The intermediate `type:"compacted"` and `event_msg/context_compacted` lines are NOT `task_complete` and are skipped by the parser (the `compacted` record has no recognized `type`/`role` mapping; `context_compacted` falls through the `event_msg` switch's skip branch), so they produce no spurious assistant_message and therefore no working→ready→working flicker. The session sits in `working` for the full ~8.3s compaction round and lands cleanly in `ready` at `task_complete`.

### Same-session continuity (vs claudecode)

Unlike newer Claude Code (≥2.1.x), which opens a NEW transcript file at the compaction boundary, codex APPENDS the compaction turn to the existing rollout — the boundary stays within one session_id. So the spec asserts one continuous session, two turns (pre- and post-compaction), with no new_session and no session-stitching dependency.

### Why irrlicht_observes is `yes`, not `partial`

The scenario's user-observable signal is the state arc (`ready → working → ready` across the compaction turn, no intervening waiting/flicker). The probe confirms the daemon emits exactly that arc via the same `task_complete`→`turn_done` path it uses for any normal codex turn. The compaction turn is ~8.3s — well above any sub-second debounce-coalescing window, so the working blip is visibly observable. The matrix's prior `daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready` is resolved upward to `yes`.

Residual caveat: irrlicht cannot LABEL the turn as a compaction (the domain CompactionState field is dead code; the `context_compacted` event is skipped rather than recorded). The dashboard shows compaction as an ordinary working turn. The spec only asserts the state arc, which is fully observed, so this is a caveat — not a downgrade.
caveats: irrlicht cannot distinguish a compaction turn from a normal long working turn: the parser skips the `type:"compacted"` and `event_msg/context_compacted` records, and the domain CompactionState field has no production writer. This is fine for the spec contract (state arc only); a 'show compaction in UI' feature would be a separate ticket.

codex's capabilities.json lists `context_curation: "unknown"`. The live probe proves the feature ships and emits structured turn events, so the honest discovery value is `true`. capabilities.json is maintainer-owned discovery data and is not edited by this assessment.

sources.note: Lines 155-172: event_msg/task_complete is the one event_msg type the parser maps to turn_done (firing IsAgentDone via the primary path); all other event_msg payloads (task_started, token_count, context_compacted) are skipped, so the compaction turn produces no flicker — just one clean working→ready arc at task_complete.

Live-probe rollout: /compact emitted task_started (21:34:39.814Z) → compacted/context_compacted → task_complete (21:34:48.110Z), a ~8.3s turn appended to the SAME rollout/session as the prior normal turn.

strings scan found 'Use /compact when the conversation gets long to summarize history and free up context.', method 'thread/compact/start', notification 'thread/compacted', and thread-item type 'contextCompaction' — primary evidence the feature ships in this build.

preconditions: codex CLI on PATH and authenticated (ChatGPT login or API key)
irrlichd is recording (--attach against a daemon with --record, or isolated mode)
codex version supports /compact (≥ 0.130.0 ships it as a slash command)
setup: Driver launches the codex TUI in tmux under a fresh per-run cwd (<staging>/cwd) and accepts the trust dialog; codex assigns its own rollout session UUID.

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok

verify: events.jsonl shows the rollout session reconciling into ready after launch (pre-session proc-PID, then the rollout session_id)
events.jsonl shows ready→working→ready for the priming turn
/compact runs as a turn: events.jsonl shows a single ready→working→ready arc on the SAME rollout session_id (codex appends compaction to the existing rollout — no new session)
no state_transition to waiting appears anywhere in the recording (no waiting/flicker around compaction)
final session state is ready; process_exited fires on the rollout session at REPL teardown

transcript.jsonl contains the priming turn (task_started/task_complete) and the compaction turn whose terminal task_complete is preceded by a context_compacted event_msg; the compaction-vs-normal-turn distinction is not surfaced by the daemon

metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready

(Reassessment of opencode CLI v1.14.50 against the current daemon and the current `drive-opencode-interactive.sh`. The prior verdict was `driver_capability: gap:slash-command`; that gap has since been closed — see "Driver" below. This flips the cell from driver-gap to record.)

## Reasoning

### Agent supports it (agent_supports: yes)

opencode v1.14.50 ships in-session compaction in two forms:

- **Manual** — a compaction command registered as `command.session.compact` (found verbatim in the v1.14.50 binary strings), with the `/summarize` slash alias also present as a literal, and a `session_compact` keybind (default `<leader>c`) documented on the official keybinds page. So the user can run one normal turn to populate context, then trigger compaction in place, then continue a second turn on the same session — exactly the `context_curation` primitive the scenario exercises.
- **Automatic** — on token overflow, opencode's `isOverflow` check (model context limit minus a reserved buffer) fires after an LLM step and the session loop inserts a `CompactionPart` into a new user message to queue summarization (`experimental.compaction.autocontinue` / `"compaction": { "auto": true }` config keys are present in the binary). This is the auto path the scenario lists as a co-trigger.

Either way compaction is an in-place LLM summarization round on the SAME session — no new session is minted (cf. the session-resume cell, which IS a fresh-transcript boundary; compaction is not).

### Daemon observes it (daemon_capability: full)

opencode is the lone `ProcessOwnedStore` adapter (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/opencode/agent.go`): the daemon reads session state straight from the SQLite store via the dedicated watcher (`watcher.go`) rather than tailing a transcript. The watcher subscribes to `opencode.db-wal` writes and, on each write, queries the `part` table for new/updated rows in known sessions and emits `EventActivity`; `isTerminalPart` recognizes a `step-finish` part and the parser maps `reason="stop"` to `turn_done` (`parser.go` `parseStepFinish`). A compaction round runs a summarization LLM step that lands a `step-finish` part on the existing session, so it surfaces to irrlicht as an ordinary `ready → working → ready` arc on that session — precisely the User-observable signal the scenario asserts (`ready → working` at compaction start; stays `working`; `working → ready` when done; no further transitions). The `CompactionPart` itself is an unrecognized part type, and the parser's `default` arm (`parser.go` lines 81-85) sets `Skip = true`, so it is harmless — it neither breaks the scan nor adds a spurious transition. The daemon cannot LABEL the turn as compaction (irrlicht's `CompactionState` domain field has no production writer), but the scenario asserts only the lifecycle arc, which IS observable. The daemon is not the blocker.

### Driver can drive it (driver_capability: ready) — THE CHANGE THIS REASSESSMENT MAKES

The prior assessment set `driver_capability: gap:slash-command` because `drive-opencode-interactive.sh` then handled only `send|wait_turn|sleep` over headless `opencode run`, and headless `opencode run` stores a `/summarize` as literal user text rather than executing it. That unblock action — "add a live-REPL/slash primitive to the opencode driver (tmux send-keys, like claudecode/codex/pi)" — has been implemented. The current driver has a `run_live()` path (lines 99-294) that launches the opencode TUI under tmux, waits for the "Ask anything" input affordance, and drives it with `tmux send-keys`. It implements a `slash` step type (lines 205-210: `tmux send-keys -l -- "$s"` then Enter) and a `reset_session` step, and the top-level router (lines 354-357) sends any script containing a `slash` (or `reset_session`) step to that live-TUI path. A recipe for this cell can therefore: `send` a priming turn (live path's `oc_send` + `oc_wait_turn` polling `step-finish` totals), then issue a `slash` step delivering `/summarize` (or the leader keybind via a `keys`-style send) inside the running REPL, then `send` a second turn — all on one session. The driver now has every step type the recipe needs; the gap is closed.

## What changed vs. the prior verdict

- agent_supports: yes → yes (unchanged; v1.14.50 confirmed `/summarize` + `command.session.compact` + `<leader>c` keybind + auto-compaction).
- daemon_capability: full → full (unchanged; re-grounded in watcher.go + parser.go for v1.14.50).
- driver_capability: gap:slash-command → **ready** (the live-TUI tmux `slash`/`reset_session` path now exists in drive-opencode-interactive.sh).

Route moves from `driver-gap` to `record`. The committed `expected.jsonl` already documents the target arc.

## Confidence

0.82: the driver's live-TUI `slash` primitive is read directly from the current driver source (router at lines 354-357, `slash` case at 205-210); daemon observability is grounded in watcher.go/parser.go; v1.14.50 ships `/summarize` + `command.session.compact` per the binary strings and the keybinds doc. Not higher only because the end-to-end live `/summarize` round has not yet been recorded against the small local model, and the exact in-TUI trigger (slash vs leader keybind) the recipe will fire is a recipe-stage detail — both forms are drivable by the live path.
caveats: irrlicht's CompactionState domain field is dead code (no production writer) — the daemon cannot distinguish a compaction turn from a normal long turn. The scenario asserts only the visible state arc, which IS observable; the compaction-vs-normal-turn label is not.

Manual compaction may surface as the `/summarize` slash command, the `command.session.compact` palette entry, or the `<leader>c` (session_compact) keybind. The driver's live-TUI path can deliver a `/summarize` slash via send-keys; if the running v1.14.50 build maps manual compaction only to the leader keybind, the recipe must send that key chord instead (still within the existing live-TUI send-keys primitive — not a new gap).

headless `opencode run` still cannot deliver in-TUI compaction (it stores slash text literally), so the recipe MUST carry a `slash` (or `reset_session`) step to force the driver onto the live-TUI tmux path; a send-only recipe would not trigger compaction.

sources.note: run_live() (lines 99-294) drives the opencode TUI under tmux; implements a `slash` step (lines 205-210: tmux send-keys -l + Enter) and reset_session; router (lines 354-357) sends any slash/reset_session script to the live path. This is the live-REPL/slash primitive whose absence drove the prior gap:slash-command verdict — it now exists, so driver_capability: ready.

lone ProcessOwnedStore adapter; watcher subscribes to opencode.db-wal and emits EventActivity from new `part` rows; isTerminalPart recognizes step-finish. A compaction summarization step lands a step-finish on the SAME session → ready→working→ready arc observed.

parseStepFinish maps reason=stop → turn_done (working→ready); the default arm (lines 81-85) Skips unrecognized part types, so a CompactionPart is harmless and adds no spurious transition — daemon_capability: full.

binary contains `command.session.compact`, the `/summarize` literal, `experimental.compaction.autocontinue`, `"compaction": { "auto": true`, and compaction-part DOM slots — manual + auto compaction both shipped in v1.14.50.
session_compact keybind (default <leader>c) — official confirmation manual compaction is a triggerable in-TUI command, deliverable via the driver's live-TUI send-keys path.

auto-compaction via isOverflow (context limit minus reserved buffer); inserts a CompactionPart into a new user message to queue summarization — confirms the auto path and the compaction-part storage shape the parser Skips.

preconditions: opencode on PATH and authenticated (~/.local/share/opencode/auth.json) — verified opencode 1.14.50. The compaction arc is model-agnostic; the dev env's default is a local LM Studio model (gemma-4-e2b), which is fine but slow (~30-60s/turn).
irrlichd is recording — run-cell.sh isolated/coexist mode, or --attach against a daemon started with --record.

LIVE-TUI cell: drive-opencode-interactive.sh routes any recipe carrying a `slash` step to its tmux TUI path (run_live), because headless `opencode run` stores a slash command as literal prompt text. /summarize must execute IN the live REPL to run the summarization round. tmux must be on PATH.
opencode version with /summarize manual compaction — confirmed v1.14.50 (command.session.compact + /summarize slash literal + session_compact keybind in the binary).
setup: Driver launches the opencode TUI in a fresh per-run cwd under <staging>/cwd/; opencode keys sessions on the cwd in its SQLite store, isolating this run's rows even alongside other opencode use.

opencode mints its own ses_… for the session; both priming turns AND the /summarize compaction round happen IN PLACE on that one ses_… (no new session — distinct from session-reset's /new). The driver enumerates the cwd's top-level ses_… rows; in-place compaction leaves exactly one, so the multi-session contract (session.uuids / transcript.paths) carries a single session.

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: one
Reply with exactly the word: two

verify: events.jsonl shows the ses_… session entering ready after launch, then two priming turns (working→ready, 'one' then 'two') on the SAME ses_…

after /summarize runs, events.jsonl shows ANOTHER ready→working→ready arc on the SAME ses_… (the summarization round is an in-place LLM turn that lands a step-finish reason=stop part — opencode compacts in place, NO new session)
no state_transition to waiting appears anywhere in the recording
after the compaction arc the session holds ready (no stuck working); final live state is ready
exactly ONE distinct ses_… session_id appears (in-place compaction mints no new session — distinct from session-reset's /new)
recipe.notes: opencode in-place compaction via the /summarize slash command. The whole observable is THREE working→ready arcs on ONE ses_…: two priming turns ('one','two') populate context, then /summarize runs an in-place LLM summarization round that surfaces as a third working→ready arc. opencode is the lone ProcessOwnedStore adapter — the daemon reads per-turn state from the SQLite store (watcher.go scanParts), and parser.go's parseStepFinish maps the summarization step-finish (reason=stop) to turn_done, so the daemon sees an ordinary working→ready arc on the SAME session. irrlicht's CompactionState field is dead code, so the compaction-vs-normal-turn LABEL is not surfaced — the recipe asserts only the visible state arc. WHY a `slash` step and NOT a `send`: headless `opencode run -- "/summarize"` stores /summarize as literal user prompt text (no command effect); the driver routes any `slash` step to run_live() which delivers it to the live TUI via tmux send-keys -l + Enter (oc_flush composer-clear confirmation). WHY NO wait_turn AFTER /summarize: the driver's `slash` arm does NOT re-snapshot TURN_BASELINE (only `send` does), so a wait_turn after a slash would compare the terminal step-finish total against the SECOND priming turn's baseline — already exceeded — and return immediately (a no-op), never actually waiting for the summarization round. Worse, embedding a send-after-slash re-introduces the slash→send composer-coalescing race that degraded checkpoint-rewind to applicable:false. So /summarize is the LAST input and is followed by a generous 75s trailing sleep — long enough for the slow local model's summarization round to complete (~30-60s) and for the daemon to observe the full working→ready arc plus the post-compaction ready dwell before tmux teardown. WHY manual /summarize (not auto-compaction): auto-compaction fires only on context-window overflow (isOverflow) and is not deterministically reproducible in a bounded recording; manual /summarize runs the identical summarization round deterministically. SINGLE session, no multi-session chain (unlike session-reset's /new): in-place compaction leaves exactly one ses_… for the cwd. What differs between runs: PID/timestamps/token counts, the assistant wording of the priming replies, the summary text, and the per-turn wall-clock latency (~30-60s on the local model). What must NOT differ: three working→ready arcs on one ses_… session (two priming + one compaction), no waiting, ending ready. Where to look if it broke: core/adapters/inbound/agents/opencode/parser.go (parseStepFinish reason=stop → turn_done) and watcher.go (scanParts step-finish detection); if /summarize stopped producing a terminal step-finish (e.g. an opencode update routed it through CompactionPart without a stop reason), the compaction arc would not register and the cell would need re-assessment.

metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## agent_supports: yes

Pi 0.70.0 ships a manual `/compact` slash command (registered in dist/core/slash-commands.js as `name: "compact"`; the CHANGELOG references it directly — "Fixed `/compact` to reuse the session thinking level for compaction summaries"). It triggers a real LLM summarization round and, on completion, writes a single top-level `{"type":"compaction","summary":...,"firstKeptEntryId":...,"tokensBefore":...}` entry to the session via SessionManager.appendCompaction() → _appendEntry() (dist/core/session-manager.js:617-621). Crucially the compact path calls _appendEntry on the CURRENT leaf — there is NO newSession()/createSessionId() in it, so compaction stays inside the SAME rollout file and the SAME session UUID. (Pi also supports automatic compaction on context-window pressure via CompactionSettings/CompactionEnabled, but that is NOT deterministically reproducible in a bounded recording; the manual `/compact` is the deterministic trigger this cell uses.)

Empirically confirmed by a standalone driver probe (2026-05-25, pi 0.70.0): send 'ok' → wait_turn → /compact → send 'still-here'. ONE session file (`...02-34-02-757Z_019e5cfb...jsonl`, single id throughout) with 8 lines: session header, model_change, thinking_level_change, user, assistant/stop (priming turn), `{type:"compaction",summary}` at 02:34:43 (~8.3s after the priming stop — a real model round), then user + assistant/stop (post-compaction turn). No second file, no waiting, no supersession.

## daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

The pi parser handles compaction explicitly (core/adapters/inbound/agents/pi/parser.go:55-60): `eventType == "compaction"` → `ev.EventType = "assistant"`, i.e. active model work that promotes ready→working (covered by TestParser_Compaction_IsActivityEvent and TestParser_Compaction_SetsLastEventToAssistant). Since the compaction line is separated from the priming ready by several seconds, the daemon emits it as its OWN working→ready arc on the same session — exactly the scenario's user-observable signal (state arc across the compaction, no intervening waiting, session continues). Pi Source = FilesUnderRoot watching ~/.pi/agent/sessions (agent.go), and the compaction entry lands in the existing rollout file, so there is no new session_id and no transcript-stitching dependency.

Why `partial`, not `yes`: irrlicht cannot LABEL the turn as a compaction. The parser collapses the compaction event into a generic assistant/working activity; the domain CompactionState field (session.go:16-18, 550) has no production writer and StringState's compaction branch is dead code, so the dashboard shows compaction as an ordinary working turn — indistinguishable from a normal long turn. The state ARC is fully observed (hence applicable: yes); the compaction-vs-normal-turn DISTINCTION is not (hence irrlicht_observes downgraded to partial). The spec asserts only the arc, which is the user-observable contract.
caveats: irrlicht cannot distinguish a compaction turn from a normal working turn: parser.go maps the `compaction` event to a generic assistant/working activity, and the domain CompactionState field is dead code (no production writer; StringState's compaction branch never fires). The dashboard shows compaction as an ordinary working turn. This is fine for the spec contract (state arc only); surfacing compaction in the UI would be a separate ticket — the reason irrlicht_observes is `partial` not `yes`.

Pi also supports AUTOMATIC compaction on context-window pressure (CompactionSettings/CompactionEnabled), but auto-compaction is not deterministically reproducible in a short bounded recording. This cell uses the MANUAL `/compact` slash command, which is deterministic and produces the same `{type:"compaction"}` entry on the same session.

DRIVER-SYNC: pi's `/compact` writes a `compaction` entry, NOT an assistant `stopReason==stop` message, so drive-pi-interactive.sh's turn-counter does not increment for it. The recipe deliberately omits a counted `wait_turn` after `/compact` (which would inflate EXPECTED_TURNS and force a driver timeout) and instead sleeps over the compaction round, then issues the post-compaction prompt with a trailing sleep to flush its turn before teardown. No driver extension is needed — only `send`/`slash`/`wait_turn`/`sleep`, all of which the pi driver already implements.

sources.note: `name: "compact"` is a registered slash command in pi 0.70.0 — primary evidence the manual /compact trigger ships.

Lines 617-621 appendCompaction(): writes a {type:"compaction",summary,firstKeptEntryId,tokensBefore,...} entry via _appendEntry on the CURRENT leaf — no newSession()/createSessionId(), so compaction stays inside the same rollout file / same session UUID (no boundary file, no supersession).

Lines 55-60: eventType=="compaction" → EventType="assistant" (active model work, ready→working). Confirmed by TestParser_Compaction_IsActivityEvent and TestParser_Compaction_SetsLastEventToAssistant. The compaction-vs-normal distinction is NOT surfaced — the event collapses into a generic assistant activity.

Lines 16-18, 550, 598-601: CompactionState constants + field + StringState branch exist but have NO production writer — dead code. The daemon cannot label a turn as compaction; hence irrlicht_observes is partial.

Live probe rollout (2026-05-25): one session id throughout; priming user+assistant/stop, then a {type:"compaction"} entry at 02:34:43 (~8.3s after priming stop), then post-compaction user+assistant/stop. No new file, no waiting.

context-compaction set to applicable: yes at confidence 0.80; pi supports manual /compact, the parser maps the compaction event to assistant(working) (no new session, no waiting). Spec asserts the lifecycle arc; the compaction label is not phase-expressible (CompactionState dead code) so it is documented in spec notes.

preconditions: pi CLI on PATH and authenticated (PI_API_KEY or configured provider; verified against pi 0.70.0)
irrlichd is recording (--attach against a daemon with --record, or isolated mode)
pi version ships the manual /compact slash command (verified live against pi 0.70.0: registered in dist/core/slash-commands.js as name:"compact")
setup: Driver boots a fresh pi REPL in a per-run cwd under <staging>/cwd/; pi assigns its own session UUID per rollout (one rollout file for the whole scenario)

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok
Reply with exactly the word: still-here

verify: events.jsonl shows a proc-<PID> pre-session born ready at launch, then the UUID rollout session reconciling into ready
events.jsonl shows ready→working→ready for the priming turn on the rollout session

after /compact runs, events.jsonl shows ANOTHER ready→working→ready arc on the SAME rollout session_id (the compaction entry is parsed as assistant/working activity — pi appends it to the existing rollout, NO new session)
events.jsonl shows ready→working→ready for the post-compaction turn on the same session (the session keeps working after compaction)
no state_transition to waiting appears anywhere in the recording
exactly one process_exited on the rollout session at REPL teardown; final session state is ready

transcript.jsonl is a SINGLE rollout file (one session id) containing the priming turn (user + assistant/stop), a {type:"compaction",summary} entry, and the post-compaction turn (user + assistant/stop); the compaction-vs-normal-turn distinction is not surfaced by the daemon
recipe.notes: Pi difference vs claudecode: pi's /compact appends a single top-level {type:"compaction",summary,...} entry to the SAME rollout file (session-manager.js appendCompaction → _appendEntry on the current leaf — NO newSession()), so the whole scenario stays on ONE session UUID — there is no boundary transcript (unlike claudecode 2.1.x) and no supersession (unlike pi /new). The pi parser (parser.go:55-60) maps the compaction event to EventType:"assistant" (active model work, ready→working), so the daemon sees it as an ordinary working→ready arc on the same session. irrlicht cannot LABEL the turn as compaction (CompactionState is dead code; the parser collapses it into a generic assistant activity), so the recipe asserts only the visible state arc — priming working→ready, a compaction working→ready arc on the same session, post-compaction working→ready, no waiting. DRIVER-SYNC (load-bearing): /compact writes a `compaction` entry, NOT an assistant stopReason==stop message, so drive-pi-interactive.sh's turn-counter does NOT increment for it. A counted wait_turn after /compact would inflate EXPECTED_TURNS by 1 and force a driver timeout (proven by a standalone probe). So the recipe deliberately uses NO wait_turn after /compact: it sleeps 14s over the real ~8s compaction summarization round (separated from the priming ready by a 3s sleep so the compaction registers as its OWN working blip), then issues the post-compaction prompt and ends on a 7s trailing sleep (NO final wait_turn) to flush that turn's working→ready arc and the post-turn ready dwell before the tmux teardown. Only send/slash/wait_turn/sleep are used — all already implemented by the pi driver; no driver extension needed. WHY interactive (not headless pi --print): a slash command only exists inside the REPL; headless pi is single-shot. WHY manual /compact (not auto): automatic compaction fires only on context-window pressure and is not deterministically reproducible in a bounded recording; manual /compact produces the identical {type:"compaction"} entry deterministically. C7 / SESSION-IDENTITY: session_birth matches the transient proc-<PID> pre-session born at launch; prime_start anchors to recording start with NO session pin so the matcher binds the FIRST working (necessarily the rollout, since the pre-session never goes working); compaction_start anchors to prime_end so it binds the post-priming working arc; every later phase pins to prime_start via same_session_as. Between runs: assistant wording, the summary text, per-turn/compaction token counts, session UUID, PID and timestamps differ; pi may flush a turn as one OR two bursts — the structural baseline is a priming working→ready arc + a compaction working→ready arc + a post-compaction working→ready arc on one rollout session_id, no waiting, a single process_exited, final ready. If it broke, look at core/adapters/inbound/agents/pi/parser.go (eventType=="compaction" → assistant) and whether a pi update changed where /compact writes the compaction entry (e.g. into a new session file).
